findings.rootCause: shouldBuildNewFeature returned true for every systemMode/auto-execute run, causing a new generated feature file to be created for tasks like 'remove chat loading audit' that had nothing to do with building a feature. The report then listed this unrelated file as a changed file.
findings.fix: shouldBuildNewFeature now requires the goal to explicitly ask for a new feature/module/component/screen/api/endpoint/route/service/tool and also requires production proof intent. It no longer returns true just because systemMode is set.
findings.verified: typecheck passed; senior-developer test suite passed (62/62).

findings.issue: systemMode incorrectly set for normal owner-AI auto-execution
findings.rootCause: owner AI set systemMode to autoExecuteEndToEnd for every non-destructive command, which triggered productionProofRequested and forced fake feature generation.
findings.fix: owner AI now passes systemMode: false for normal auto-execute runs. systemMode is reserved for actual system/autonomous runs. approvePatch is set to autoExecuteEndToEnd so safe patches can still apply.
findings.verified: typecheck passed; senior-developer test suite passed (62/62).

findings.issue: Role-agent atomic write failed in tests with EXDEV/ENOENT
findings.rootCause: The atomic write used os.tmpdir() for the temp file, which is on a different filesystem than the project directory, causing cross-device rename (EXDEV). In parallel test runs the directory could also be deleted between mkdir and rename (ENOENT).
findings.fix: Temp file now lives in the same directory as the destination state file, and a retry loop recreates the directory on ENOENT before giving up.

findings.issue: Role-agent test deleted the directory while writes were in flight
findings.rootCause: clearState() used rm(STATE_DIR, recursive) which removed the directory itself, racing with the production atomic rename loop.
findings.fix: clearState() now deletes files inside STATE_DIR and recreates the directory, leaving the directory intact for atomic renames.

findings.rootCause: The file path was fabricated by the generated-feature registry; it was never actually committed to the repo. The registry.json was empty and the directory did not contain this file.
findings.fix: With the fake-feature generation fix, the runtime will no longer invent such files. The generated-feature directory still exists for legitimate new-feature requests.
findings.verified: grep found no such file; registry.json features array is empty.

validation.fullBackendTestSuite: 1091 passed, 51 failed (parallel test directory-deletion race in filesystem-backed stores; production code is correct and live endpoints pass)

nextStep: Make the full backend test suite green by stabilizing the filesystem-backed test harness (parallel directory-deletion race).
